policy: Version = `<ponder-version>-sqd.<rev>`: @subsquid/ponder@X.Y.Z-sqd.N is built from ponder@X.Y.Z + the Portal layer (rev N). The ponder version stays visible; bump `-sqd.<rev>` for a fork-side fix on the SAME ponder version (npm permanently retires an unpublished version number, so a plain mirror like 0.16.6 can't be re-cut after a bad publish). The NEWEST ponder version is published with `--tag latest` (the default `npm install`); an older version publishes under a `ponder-<version>` dist-tag instead of clobbering `latest`. The bin stays `ponder` (drop-in). We cut a release only for ponder versions a client needs; `compat.tested` records which versions the seam is proven against.
nextFromMain: main carries the full correctness wave — the invariant-first re-architecture of portal/ (functional core + imperative shell, INV-1…INV-18, portal/INVARIANTS.md) plus the fix stream through the #47 mid-range-204 phantom-coverage close, the #50 bounded time-to-first-durable-commit (quantum warmup, resume-seeded fetch, geometric discovery slow-start), and the #20/#94 extend needed-field scoping. Behavior + seam frozen; the full Portal property/regression suite (302 tests) passes on 0.15.17/0.16.6/0.16.7. This wave re-cuts 0.15.17 and 0.16.6 as -sqd.2 and adds 0.16.7-sqd.1. DO NOT publish from this PR — publish is a separate, deliberate step.
seam: HistoricalSync.syncBlockRangeData / syncBlockData (interval-scoped) — identical in shape across the tested range

versions.note: 0.17.0-sqd.1 grafts the current Portal layer (invariant-first re-architecture INV-1…INV-18 + the full correctness wave through the #47 mid-range-204 fix, the #50 bounded-time-to-first-commit warmup, the #20/#94 needed-field scoping, the #110/#111 access_list honest-NULL fix, the #23 realtime body-cap filtered fallback, the #175 INV-17 realtime-finalize write-side idempotence, and the #143 inserted.logs store-inserted metric) onto ponder@0.17.0. wiring/0.17.0.patch is RE-DERIVED, NOT a verbatim copy: 0.16.10→0.17.0 is a tree-wide MECHANICAL reformat (import-list reorder, `static readonly`→`static override readonly`, `@ts-ignore`→`@ts-expect-error`) plus dep bumps (@ponder/utils 0.2.18→0.3.0, pg ^8.11→^8.16). Applying the 0.16.10 patch left 29 of 34 hunks landing by offset and 5 rejected — ALL 5 pure import-block additions (rpc/actions.ts, runtime/historical.ts, runtime/index.ts, runtime/realtime.ts) where the reformat reordered the surrounding import lists; each rejected hunk's added (+) lines were re-placed BYTE-FOR-BYTE into the reformatted import block (comm-verified: the set of added wiring lines is identical to 0.16.10.patch, 543 lines, zero delta). The regenerated patch is 12 files / 35 hunks vs 0.16.10's 12/34 — the extra hunk is a benign context-driven SPLIT of the actions.ts import edits (0.16.10's 2 import hunks became 3 as the reformat inserted context between the edit regions); no wiring line is dropped or added. The graft seam (HistoricalSync.syncBlockRangeData/syncBlockData, interval-scoped) survives UNTOUCHED. It builds (tsc no error; @ponder/utils 0.3.0 + pg ^8.16 compile with NO fork dep change — the fork inherits upstream deps as-is) and passes the full current Portal suite (370 tests, 19 files) on ponder@0.17.0. As the newest tracked ponder version it will publish with --tag latest when released; publishing is a separate, deliberate, human-reserved step, so this row stays published:false and 0.16.9-sqd.1 remains latest until then. The one-command gate (scripts/sync-upstream.sh 0.17.0 --test) runs start-to-finish and passes: ponder@0.17.0 bumps `engines.pnpm` to `>=11` and `packageManager` to pnpm@11.0.0 (0.16.10 had no engines.pnpm and packageManager pnpm@9.10.0), so the build needs pnpm 11 — the harness now reads the clone's own `packageManager` right after the clone (`PNPM="corepack ${package.json.packageManager:-pnpm@9.10.0}"`) and builds each version with the pnpm it declares, falling back to the historical pnpm@9.10.0 pin when the field is absent (verified non-regressing: 0.16.10 still builds with pnpm@9.10.0, full 370-test suite green under both versions).

0.16.10-sqd.1 grafts the current Portal layer (invariant-first re-architecture INV-1…INV-18 + the full correctness wave through the #47 mid-range-204 fix, the #50 bounded-time-to-first-commit warmup, the #20/#94 needed-field scoping, the #110/#111 access_list honest-NULL fix, the #23 realtime body-cap filtered fallback, the #175 INV-17 realtime-finalize write-side idempotence, and the #143 inserted.logs store-inserted metric) onto ponder@0.16.10. wiring/0.16.10.patch is a verbatim copy of wiring/0.16.9.patch (sha256 19743650…): 0.16.9→0.16.10 is a three-file upstream change — runtime/isolated.ts, runtime/multichain.ts, runtime/omnichain.ts only — wrapping the per-event checkpoint-update + pglite live-query TRUNCATE in a try/catch that invalidates+clears the in-memory indexing cache before rethrowing (a commit-path resilience fix). Those edits sit in the per-event processing loop (isolated ~L643, multichain ~L732, omnichain ~L752), ~500 lines below the run* entry-point region (~L152-177) the wiring patch modifies — non-overlapping, so the patch applies with zero rejects. It builds and passes the full current Portal suite (370 tests) on ponder@0.16.10 (scripts/sync-upstream.sh 0.16.10 --test). The changed region is off the graft seam (HistoricalSync.syncBlockRangeData/syncBlockData, untouched), so the fork passively inherits the upstream cache-invalidation resilience fix. No fresh RPC byte-diff on 0.16.10; compat rests on seam-identity + the full suite, as for 0.16.9/0.16.8/0.15.17. As the newest tracked ponder version it will publish with --tag latest when released; publishing is a separate, deliberate, human-reserved step, so this row stays published:false and 0.16.9-sqd.1 remains latest until then. (Upstream 0.17.0 exists but is a tree-wide mechanical reformat — import reorder, `static override`, `@ts-ignore`→`@ts-expect-error` — plus @ponder/utils 0.3.0 + pg ^8.16 dep bumps; the seam survives but the wiring patch needs hunk-by-hunk re-derivation, tracked separately, not a verbatim copy like 0.16.10.)

0.16.9-sqd.1 grafts the current Portal layer (invariant-first re-architecture INV-1…INV-18 + the correctness wave: the #47 mid-range-204 fix, the #50 bounded-time-to-first-commit warmup, the #20/#94 needed-field scoping, the #110/#111 access_list honest-NULL fix, and the #23 realtime body-cap filtered fallback) onto ponder@0.16.9. wiring/0.16.9.patch is a verbatim copy of wiring/0.16.8.patch: 0.16.8→0.16.9 is a single-file upstream change — src/indexing-store/cache.ts only (ponder-sh/ponder#2323: fix p.bytes() bytea encoding on the Postgres COPY flush path, emitting \x<hex> for Buffer values to fix `DelayedInsertError: invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8": 0x00`) — and every upstream file the wiring patch modifies is byte-identical between 0.16.8 and 0.16.9, so the patch applies with zero rejects. It builds and passes the full current Portal suite (328 tests) on ponder@0.16.9 (scripts/sync-upstream.sh 0.16.9 --test). indexing-store/cache.ts is off the graft surface (sync-historical, runtime/historical, sync-store, rpc, sync, sync-realtime), so the seam is untouched and the fork passively inherits the upstream bytea-COPY correctness fix. No fresh RPC byte-diff on 0.16.9; compat rests on seam-identity + the full suite, as for 0.16.8/0.16.7/0.15.17. It was published as 0.16.9-sqd.1 with --tag latest on 2026-07-14 and is now the default `npm install @subsquid/ponder`, superseding 0.16.8-sqd.1 as latest; it remains latest until a newer tracked version (0.16.10) is deliberately published.

0.16.8-sqd.1 is cut from the SAME Portal layer as 0.16.7-sqd.1 (invariant-first re-architecture INV-1…INV-18 + the correctness wave through the #47 mid-range-204 fix, the #50 bounded-time-to-first-commit warmup, the #20/#94 needed-field scoping, and the #110/#111 access_list honest-NULL fix), grafted onto ponder@0.16.8. The wiring patch is byte-identical to 0.16.6/0.16.7 (sha256 ddd12dd5…, same 10/10 files) — it applies cleanly (zero rejects), builds, and passes the full current Portal suite (312 tests — grown from the 302 at the 0.16.7 cut as the correctness wave added coverage) on ponder@0.16.8 (scripts/sync-upstream.sh 0.16.8 --test). 0.16.7→0.16.8 is a realtime-only upstream change: it adds isAsyncExecutionChain (chainId 143/10143) in sync-realtime/index.ts + utils/finality.ts so async-execution chains can surface blocks before logsBloom is execution-ready — neither file is on the graft surface (sync-historical, runtime/historical, sync-store, rpc, sync), so the seam is untouched and the 81 Portal realtime tests (portal-realtime-wire/portal-realtime/realtime-standardize) pass unchanged. No fresh RPC byte-diff on 0.16.8; compat rests on seam-identity + the full suite, as for 0.16.7/0.15.17. As the newest ponder version it publishes with --tag latest; it was published as 0.16.8-sqd.1 on 2026-07-10 and is now the default `npm install @subsquid/ponder`, superseding 0.16.7-sqd.1 as latest. 0.16.7-sqd.1 remains published and installable by exact version; no dist-tag currently points at it (it was latest before 0.16.8-sqd.1, and npm does not auto-retag a superseded version — a ponder-0.16.7 tag would have to be added deliberately).

0.16.7-sqd.1 is cut from the SAME Portal layer as 0.16.6-sqd.2 (invariant-first re-architecture INV-1…INV-18 + the correctness wave through the #47 mid-range-204 fix, the #50 bounded-time-to-first-commit warmup, and the #20/#94 needed-field scoping), grafted onto ponder@0.16.7. Seam identical to 0.16.6 — the 0.16.6 wiring patch applies cleanly (zero rejects, 10/10 files), builds, and passes the full Portal suite (302 tests) on ponder@0.16.7 (CI matrix). 0.16.6→0.16.7 is one DB-layer PR (ponder-sh/ponder#2314: live-query notification batching, drops the 100-table limit) touching database/actions, drizzle/onchain, build/schema, bin/createViews — none of the graft surfaces (sync-historical, runtime/historical, sync-store, rpc, sync). No fresh RPC byte-diff on 0.16.7; compat rests on seam-identity + the full suite, as for 0.15.17. Published as 0.16.7-sqd.1 with --tag latest; superseded as latest by 0.16.8-sqd.1 on 2026-07-10 and now has no dist-tag (installable by exact version — a ponder-0.16.7 tag would have to be added deliberately).

0.16.6-sqd.2 re-cuts ponder@0.16.6 with the full current Portal layer: the invariant-first re-architecture (INV-1…INV-18, portal/INVARIANTS.md) plus the correctness wave through the #47 mid-range-204 close, the #50 bounded-time-to-first-commit warmup, and the #20/#94 needed-field scoping. Full Portal suite (302 tests) green on ponder@0.16.6. The earlier 0.16.6-sqd.1 (published --tag latest, byte-identical vs RPC across logs/tx/receipts/traces + the early C-series fixes) predates that wave and is superseded by this cut. No longer the newest ponder version, so this rev publishes under dist-tag ponder-0.16.6, not latest.

0.15.17-sqd.2 re-cuts ponder@0.15.17 with the full current Portal layer: the invariant-first re-architecture (INV-1…INV-18, portal/INVARIANTS.md) plus the correctness wave through the #47 mid-range-204 close, the #50 bounded-time-to-first-commit warmup, and the #20/#94 needed-field scoping. Seam identical to 0.16.6 — the 0.16.6 wiring patch applies cleanly, builds, and passes the full Portal suite (302 tests) on ponder@0.15.17 (CI matrix). The earlier 0.15.17-sqd.1 predates that wave and is superseded by this cut. Publishes under dist-tag ponder-0.15.17.

compat.seamStableRange: 0.15.17 - 0.17.0 (verified identical by inspection; CI matrix promotes to 'tested' as patches land)
compat.howToAdd: see PUBLISHING.md — scripts/sync-upstream.sh <ver> --test, then add the row here
